description: fs, path, net, child_process, and some process usages fail or get polyfilled differently in browser bundles.
body: Use when shared code is imported by browser entries. Do not add Node-only dependencies to utilities consumed by UI code. Split runtime adapters or lazy-load inside Node-only paths. Verify with bundler build and browser console. Risk includes leaking server assumptions and increasing client bundle size. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
